summary: Iteration 2 testing for Agenda Italserrande PWA covering the two new features: (1) GET /api/clients/search and (2) GET /api/advances/by-worker, plus regression of iteration 1 flows. Backend: 20/20 pytest tests pass (8 new tests added for the 2 new endpoints incl. auth gate, <2-char empty result, name/address/phone matching, sort-by-date-desc, max-50 cap, monthly aggregation totals/counts/last_date/sort, per-user isolation, empty-month, plus 12 retained regression tests for clients/expenses/advances CRUD, summary, isolation). Frontend (Playwright e2e on real preview URL): register -> agenda -> add 2 clients -> ClientSearch shows 2 results for unique token -> clicking result opens ClientFormDialog pre-filled with correct name (data-testid=client-form-dialog) -> clear button resets input -> short query (<2 chars) correctly hides dropdown -> add advance -> Riepilogo new section 'Acconti per operaio' renders worker-row-<NAME> with count and amount, and 'Totale acconti del mese' = total_advances from /api/summary (75 € == 75 €). No JS errors, only a benign a11y warning about missing aria-describedby on shadcn Dialog (pre-existing).

issues: Console a11y warning: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby={undefined} for {DialogContent}'. Add a <DialogDescription> or aria-describedby in DialogContent to silence and improve accessibility. Pre-existing, not blocking.

action_items: (Optional, low priority) Add DialogDescription/aria-describedby to shadcn Dialog wrappers to remove the a11y console warning.
critical_code_review_comments: ClientSearch.jsx correctly imports the date-fns Italian locale as `it as itLocale` — avoids the iter-1 shadowing bug. Good fix.
search_clients in server.py uses re.escape on the user term before $regex — safe against regex injection. Good.
advances_by_worker uses an aggregation pipeline scoped by user_id and a date prefix regex — natural per-month reset; per-user isolation verified.
advances/by-worker endpoint has no response_model. Fine for now since shape is plain dict, but adding a Pydantic model would harden the API contract.

Frontend Riepilogo computes 'Totale acconti del mese' from byWorker rows; this matches data.total_advances when both come from the same DB state. If a request races, they could diverge briefly — acceptable trade-off.
test_credentials: Created dynamically via Firebase REST signUp using REACT_APP_FIREBASE_API_KEY. Pattern: testuser+it2<timestamp>@italserrande.test / TestPassword123!. Two distinct users used for backend isolation tests.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests seed TEST_-prefixed clients/expenses/advances per test class and clean up in finally blocks. Frontend test creates 2 clients ('Mario <UNIQ>', 'Luigi <UNIQ>') and 1 advance ('OperaioX_<UNIQ>') under a fresh registered user — no cleanup performed on the FE side (acceptable as user is throwaway).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py now has 20 pytest tests grouped in TestAuthGates, TestClientsCRUD, TestExpensesCRUD, TestAdvancesCRUD, TestSummary, TestIsolation, TestClientSearch (5 tests), TestAdvancesByWorker (3 tests). Run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v. New iter-2 data-testids verified: client-search-input, client-search-clear, client-search-results, search-result-<id>, riepilogo-by-worker-list, worker-row-<NAME>. ClientSearch debounce is 250ms — wait ~400-600ms after typing. The advance dialog opens by data-testid='add-advance-button' but its inputs do not yet have data-testids (used by-position selectors); consider adding testids if more advance-dialog testing is needed.
rca of the issue: No new bugs found in iteration 2. The iter-1 variable-shadowing class of bug was avoided in ClientSearch.jsx by aliasing the date-fns Italian locale as `itLocale`. All new endpoints behave per the spec.
